note: Manual founder-priority cut favoring explicit speech-language profiles over behavior-therapy-heavy adjacency.

prospects.name: Peter
prospects.whyNow: Best blend of explicit speech-language positioning, high review count, and strong child-facing carryover language in the current Nigerian list.
prospects.caution: Behavior and educational therapy language is still present, so keep the outreach centered on speech carryover rather than a generic SEN tool.
prospects.angle: Lead with between-session continuity for child speech targets and visible parent follow-through.

prospects.name: Miracle
prospects.whyNow: Clear speech-therapy identity with strong child communication language and enough marketplace proof to justify immediate founder outreach.
prospects.caution: Broad challenge coverage may mean a wider caseload than pure articulation or language-delay work.
prospects.angle: Lead with therapist-controlled home practice and communication gains between sessions.

prospects.name: Kemily
prospects.whyNow: Strongest clean speech-language positioning in the shortlist outside the high-review Lagos profiles, with modern service framing and parent coaching language.
prospects.caution: No review proof or external contact recovered yet, so this is high-fit but lower-confidence commercially.
prospects.angle: Lead with structured home carryover, progress tracking, and parent coaching support.

prospects.name: Rhodiyat
prospects.whyNow: Very clean child speech-therapy positioning with explicit parent guidance and home support language.
prospects.caution: Marketplace proof is still thin, so treat as a promising early-adopter rather than a guaranteed high-volume practice.
prospects.angle: Lead with parent-facing follow-through and lightweight therapist review between sessions.

prospects.name: Adewunmi
prospects.whyNow: Explicit speech pathology and audiology framing with clear articulation, fluency, and autism-related communication scope.
prospects.caution: The profile spans children and adults, so position Wulo on the child and caregiver side of the caseload.
prospects.angle: Lead with child speech practice, articulation carryover, and therapy visibility outside live sessions.

prospects.name: Anawanti
prospects.whyNow: Explicit speech therapist wording and a clear special-needs child focus make this a good Wulo-fit despite lower marketplace proof.
prospects.caution: The profile is light on differentiated method detail, so outreach should stay practical rather than visionary.
prospects.angle: Lead with school-home carryover and simpler therapist-supervised practice for children with communication needs.

prospects.name: Akinbobloa
prospects.whyNow: Strong explicit speech identity with a school-based Abuja footprint and meaningful overlap with learning-disability and speech-delay work.
prospects.caution: There is educator crossover in the profile, so keep the framing anchored on speech therapy outcomes rather than classroom support.
prospects.angle: Lead with school-linked carryover, therapist review visibility, and child practice between sessions.

prospects.name: Prince
prospects.whyNow: Explicit speech-language therapist identity and respectable review proof put this profile above most speech-adjacent alternatives.
prospects.caution: The method copy leans toward music and vocal intervention language, so validate clinical fit during outreach.
prospects.angle: Lead with structured speech practice rather than broad therapy administration.

prospects.name: Kehinde
prospects.whyNow: Explicit audiology and speech pathology background with broad clinical coverage and strong child-speech relevance.
prospects.caution: The listed `₦10/h` price looks like a marketplace anomaly, so confirm seriousness and current practice before investing too much outreach effort.
prospects.angle: Lead with high-structure child speech carryover and therapist-controlled goal tracking.

prospects.name: Funmilola Christiana
prospects.whyNow: Not a pure speech-language profile, but the parent/home carryover language and strong review volume make it the best controlled adjacency for founder learning.
prospects.caution: This is behavior-therapy-heavy and should be treated as a secondary experiment, not the core wedge.
prospects.angle: Lead with parent follow-through and practical between-session support rather than clinical speech specialization.
